title: Fans speculate GTA 6 Trailer 3 could arrive August 7
summary: Players believe Rockstar may reveal the third trailer on August 7, tied to Take-Two's earnings call schedule. Speculation intensified after the company changed its financial reporting timing, with some fans expecting both a cinematic trailer and gameplay footage before launch. The wait has lasted over a year since the second trailer.

title: Australian players may need ID verification to play GTA 6
summary: New online safety regulations in Australia could require players to verify their age using driver's licenses or other official documents before accessing GTA 6. Rockstar faces potential fines up to $49.5 million per breach for non-compliance. The requirement applies to games with R18+ ratings under laws that took effect in March 2026.

title: Rockstar website update hints at potential trailer reveal
summary: Rockstar Games is currently updating their homepage and has removed the pre-order banner, which historically signals new announcements. While similar updates have preceded news about other games in the past, fans are speculating this could indicate the third GTA 6 trailer is imminent. No gameplay footage has been shown since the second trailer in May 2025.

title: Rockstar developers pursue unionization amid crunch period
summary: Developers at Rockstar's UK studios are moving forward with unionization efforts as the studio enters the final crunch period before GTA 6's November launch. The move follows last year's dismissal of employees who allegedly attempted to organize, though Rockstar cited misconduct. Workers are seeking protections regarding overtime, pay transparency, and flexible working conditions.

title: PlayStation boycott unlikely to impact GTA 6 launch
summary: Analysts suggest that the ongoing PlayStation Plus subscription cancellation campaign will not affect GTA 6's digital-only launch scheduled for November 19. Despite consumer protests over Sony's shift away from physical media, industry experts believe the noise is insufficient to disrupt either Sony's subscription base or the game's sales performance.
